item.title: Hantavirus risk from cruise ship low: WHO
item.description: A suspected outbreak of hantavirus on a cruise ship, on which three people have died, presents a low risk to the public, the WHO Europe said Monday, as Dutch authorities planned to repatriate two sick people....Maps

item.title: World beyond Pluto may have atmosphere: astronomers
item.description: A tiny, little-known world beyond Pluto appears to have an atmosphere, Japanese astronomers said Monday, defying what had been thought possible for icy objects in our cosmic backyard....

item.title: Elderly woman killed being hit by motorbike
item.description: A woman was killed after being struck by a motorbike at Boro Bhangha Mahakash Bypass road under Demra in Dhaka on Monday....

item.title: Russian strikes on Ukraine kill nine
item.description: A Russian missile attack killed seven people in an eastern Ukrainian town and a separate attack left two others dead in a southern village, Kyiv said Monday....

item.title: US envoy leads Bangladesh team in Washington investment summit
item.description: United States ambassador to Bangladesh Brent T Christensen is leading a 25-member Bangladeshi business delegation to the 2026 SelectUSA Investment Summit in Washington in the US, aiming to boost bilateral investment and business engagement....Diasporacommunity forum

item.title: Four migrants found dead near Croatia-Slovenia border
item.description: Four migrants were found dead near the Slovenian border and two others were taken to hospital in a serious condition after being transported by truck in ‘inhumane conditions’, Croatian police said Monday....

item.title: Corruption in ADP key barrier to economic revival: Saki
item.description: State minister for planning Zonayed Abdur Rahim Saki on Monday identified the annual development programme, plagued by corruption and leakages, as a major hindrance to recovering the fragile economy inherited by the newly elected government....

item.title: Bangladesh faces rising climate-sensitive diseases: workshop
item.description: Bangladesh is increasingly facing climate change induced health risks that include dengue, malaria and waterborne infections, speakers said at a workshop in Dhaka on Monday....

item.title: Section 144 imposed as JCD factions clash in Rangamati
item.description: Clashes erupted in Rangamati municipality area on Monday over the newly formed district committee of Jatiyatabadi Chhatra Dal, prompting the local authorities to impose Section 144 of the Code of Criminal Procedure restricting public gathering....

item.title: Cost likely to double even before start of project
item.description: The cost of the Dhaka East-West Elevated Expressway public–private partnership project is likely to double even before the project begins, project officials said....

item.title: Sony Ramani wins top prize in Nordic photo contest
item.description: New Age staff photojournalist Sony Ramani has won the first prize in a photography competition titled ‘Inclusive Societies: Free and Independent Press’....Healthand wellness

item.title: Fire Service observes Firefighters Day
item.description: Brigadier General Muhammad Jahed Kamal, director general of the Fire Service and Civil Defence Department, has called for the development of the department as a transparent, accountable and genuine service-giving institution through the collective efforts of all....

item.title: Clinic besieged over alleged incomplete surgery
item.description: A doctor at Ayesha Clinic and Diagnostic Center in the BSCIC Industrial City area of Lalmonirhat district town has been accused of leaving a surgery incomplete, triggering protests by the patient’s relatives and locals....

item.title: SMUCT extends admission fair
item.description: The duration of the ongoing admission fair 2026 for the summer semester at Shanto-Mariam University of Creative Technology has been extended....

item.title: Police submit final report, term it a case of suicide
item.description: The police have submitted a final report stating that Swarnamoyee Biswas, a graphic designer at online news portal Dhaka Stream, died by suicide due to family disputes, neglect by relatives, and prolonged mental distress....Maps

item.title: Free media key to accountability: info minister
item.description: Information and broadcasting minister Zahir Uddin Swapon said on Monday that free and responsible media were essential to ensure government accountability, urging journalists to resist propaganda and uphold democratic values....

item.title: Rehabilitation for Urdu-speaking people demanded
item.description: Speakers at a media advocacy programme on Monday called for the rehabilitation of Urdu-speaking people, commonly known as Biharis, to address their housing crisis and improve their living conditions....

item.title: Every custodial death needs to be probed independently, UN special rapporteur tells New Age
item.description: The UN special rapporteur on torture and other cruel, inhuman or degrading treatment or punishment, Alice Jill Edwards, said that every custodial death needed to be investigated independently....

item.title: Writ petition questions interim govt legality
item.description: A Supreme Court lawyer on Sunday filed a writ petition with the High Court asking the government to explain why it should not be directed to form an independent commission to investigate the alleged constitutional violations by the interim government, including......

item.title: PM orders streamlined project approval, closed factory reopening
item.description: Prime minister Tarique Rahman on Monday directed authorities to simplify the project approval process in a bid to attract both domestic and foreign investment, while pledging support for investors willing to revive long-idle state-owned factories....

item.title: Power tariff hikes likely soon
item.description: The state-owned Bangladesh Power Development Board is likely to submit a proposal to the Bangladesh Energy Regulatory Commission soon seeking upward adjustments in the rates of power for both retail and bulk consumers....Diasporacommunity forum

item.title: Depositors lock bank branches in Ctg
item.description: Depositors again on Monday locked three bank branches in Chattogram, demanding the return of their deposits with full profits....

item.title: Retaining talent for competitive edge
item.description: Organisations in Bangladesh have, in recent years, invested significantly in technology, infrastructure and expansion. However, one of the most valuable drivers of long-term growth is often overlooked: people. Experienced, skilled and dedicated employees are the backbone of any productive, innovative and customer-oriented enterprise. When such employees leave......Local news updates

item.title: Power, purpose and void between them
item.description: THERE are moments when a system continues to function long after its meaning has evaporated. The lights stay on. The machinery hums. Orders are issued, numbers are processed and announcements are made. Yet something essential — something quieter and harder to measure — has already departed......

item.title: Driving market transformation
item.description: ON A humid morning in rural Bangladesh, a farmer scrolls through market prices on a smartphone before deciding where to sell his produce. Not long ago, such a decision would have depended on local middlemen, often leaving him with little bargaining power. That quiet shift captures something larger now underway across the country. Data, connectivity and......

item.title: Global governance crisis
item.description: THE contemporary international system is undergoing a profound transformation marked by a crisis in global governance and the erosion of multilateralism. Institutions that once underpinned cooperation, such as the United Nations and the World Trade Organization, are increasingly constrained by geopolitical rivalries, nationalist politics and declining trust......Country specific content

item.title: HC should mull over code of conduct for media
item.description: JOURNALISTS covering legal proceedings at the Supreme Court held protests by covering their faces on World Press Freedom Day, demanding the restoration of their access to courtrooms. The journalists say that the restrictions on journalists’ access to the Appellate Division courtroom and several High Court benches over four months is concerning because courtrooms are......

item.title: Deplorable non-compliance with court orders
item.description: IT IS unacceptable that health authorities have largely failed to implement a number of pragmatic and necessary High Court directives issued over the years to ensure patients’ rights, accountability in medical services and the regulation of diagnostic centres, pharmaceutical companies and physicians. The failure is not merely a matter of administrative delay. It reflects......

item.title: Iranian missiles, drones downed, six boats destroyed, claims US
item.description: The hostilities occurred as US forces sought to facilitate the transit of commercial shipping through the Strait of Hormuz…...Local news updates

item.title: Housewife ‘raped’ in Cumilla, 4 held
item.description: A housewife has allegedly been raped by a group of people at Chandina in Cumilla district....Globalheadlines service

item.title: Young doctor found dead at Shahbagh
item.description: A young doctor was found dead at his shared accommodation in the capital’s Shahbagh area on Monday afternoon, shortly after he had stopped responding to phone calls from his family members....

item.title: Police to get blue shirts, khaki trousers as uniform
item.description: Home minister Salahuddin Ahmed on Monday said that the government had decided to change the police uniform, bringing back khaki pants after more than two decades....

item.title: AIUB opens AI supercomputing lab
item.description: The American International University–Bangladesh inaugurated its state-of-the-art AI supercomputing lab on its permanent campus in Dhaka on Sunday....
